---
title: "Liu (2009) Simulation Study"
author: "Shozen Dan"
date: "2025-11-23"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(cmdstanr)
library(bayesplot)

REPO_DIR <- "~/Imperial/0_Research/modular_bayes/"
```

```{r experiment-config}
set.seed(1234)

config <- list(
    n1 = 25,
    n2 = 50,
    mu = 0,
    theta = 1,
    s1 = 1,
    s2 = 1
)
```

```{r}
# Function to generate data for the simulation study
generate_data <- function(n1, n2, mu, theta, s1, s2) {
    y1 <- rnorm(n1, mean = mu, sd = s1)
    y2 <- rnorm(n2, mean = mu + theta, sd = s2)

    return(list(y1 = y1, y2 = y2))
}

set.seed(1234)
data <- generate_data(
    n1 = config$n1,
    n2 = config$n2,
    mu = config$mu,
    theta = config$theta,
    s1 = config$s1,
    s2 = config$s2
)
```

```{r sanity-check}
ggplot() +
    geom_histogram(aes(x = data$y1), bins = 15, fill = "blue", alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_histogram(aes(x = data$y2), bins = 15, fill = "red", alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(title = "Histograms of Simulated Data", x = "Value", y = "Frequency") +
    theme_bw()
```

```{r prepare-data-for-stan}
stan_data <- list(
    n1 = config$n1,
    n2 = config$n2,
    y1 = data$y1,
    y2 = data$y2
)
```

```{r fit-model, message=FALSE}
model <- cmdstan_model("../../stan/liu.stan")
fit <- model$sample(
    data = stan_data,
    chains = 4,
    parallel_chains = 4,
    iter_warmup = 1000,
    iter_sampling = 1000,
    seed = 1234
)
```

```{r posterior-summary}
posterior <- fit$draws()
summary <- fit$summary()
print(summary)
```

```{r posterior-plots}
mcmc_areas(posterior, pars = c("mu", "theta"))
```

```{r}
# Extract posterior draws
mu_draws <- as.numeric(posterior[,,"mu"])
theta_draws <- as.numeric(posterior[,,"theta"])
```

```{r}
# Analytically derived density function
library(mvtnorm)
cond_loglik_m2_true <- function(mu) {
  n2 <- length(data$y2)
  one_n2 <- rep(1, n2)
  S <- diag(n2) + 0.5 * one_n2 %o% one_n2 
  dmvnorm(data$y2, mean = rep(mu, n2), sigma = S, log = TRUE)
}
```

```{r}
# Plot the true (log) conditional marginal probability
{
  x <- seq(-3, 3, 1e-3)
  log_p_true <- sapply(x, cond_loglik_m2_true)
  
  plot(x, log_p_true, type = "l", xlab = "theta", ylab = "log(p)")
  abline(v = mean(data$y2), col=2)
}
```

```{r}
# Plot the true (log) un-normalized weights
{
  x <- seq(-3, 3, 1e-3)
  log_p_true <- sapply(x, cond_loglik_m2_true)
  
  plot(x, -log_p_true, type = "l", xlab = "theta", ylab = "log(p)")
  abline(v = mean(data$y2), col=2)
}
```

```{r}
# Sample from the prior of theta
M <- 3000
theta_prior_draws <- rnorm(M, 0, sqrt(0.5))
```

```{r}
# Precompute data and sizes once
y2   <- data$y2
n2   <- length(y2)
N_mu <- length(mu_draws)
M_th <- length(theta_prior_draws)

# Conditional log-likelihood with sigma = I
cond_loglik_m2 <- function(mu, theta) {
  mean_val <- mu + theta
  sum(dnorm(data$y2, mean = mean_val, sd = 1, log = TRUE))
}

log_mean_exp <- function(logx) {
  # log( mean(exp(logx)) ) computed stably
  m <- max(logx)
  m + log(mean(exp(logx - m)))
}

log_p <- vapply(
  mu_draws,
  FUN = function(mu_i) {
    # log-likelihoods over theta for this mu_i
    log_vals <- vapply(
      theta_prior_draws,
      FUN       = function(th) cond_loglik_m2(mu_i, th),
      FUN.VALUE = numeric(1L)
    )
    # log p(y2 | mu_i) ≈ log(1/M ∑ exp(log_vals))
    log_mean_exp(log_vals)
  },
  FUN.VALUE = numeric(1L)
)
```

```{r}
log_w = -log_p
log_sum_w <- matrixStats::logSumExp(log_w)

w_tilde <- exp(log_w - log_sum_w)

# Re-sample the posterior draws using importance sampling weights
{
  post_draws <- cbind(mu_draws, theta_draws)
  ix <- seq(1, nrow(post_draws))

  ix_redraw <- sample(ix, replace=TRUE, prob=w_tilde)

  post_redraws <- post_draws[ix_redraw,]
}
```

```{r}
cat(sprintf("Full      E[mu]:     %.4f\n", mean(mu_draws)))
cat(sprintf("Corrected E[mu]:     %.4f\n", mean(post_redraws[,1])))

cat(sprintf("Full      V[mu]:     %.4f\n", var(mu_draws)))
cat(sprintf("Corrected V[mu]:     %.4f\n", var(post_redraws[,1])))

cat(sprintf("Full      E[theta]:  %.4f\n", mean(theta_draws)))
cat(sprintf("Corrected E[theta]:  %.4f\n", mean(post_redraws[,2])))

cat(sprintf("Full      V[theta]:  %.4f\n", var(theta_draws)))
cat(sprintf("Corrected V[theta]:  %.4f\n", var(post_redraws[,2])))
```

```{r}
breaks <- pretty(c(mu_draws, post_redraws[,1]), n = 30)

hist(mu_draws,
     col = rgb(1, 0, 0, 0.5),
     border = NA,
     freq = FALSE,
     breaks = breaks,
     main = "Posterior of mu: Before vs After",
     xlab = "mu")

hist(post_redraws[,1],
     col = rgb(0, 0, 1, 0.5),
     border = NA,
     freq = FALSE,
     breaks = breaks,
     add = TRUE)
```
```{r}
breaks <- pretty(c(theta_draws, post_redraws[,2]), n = 30)

hist(theta_draws,
     col = rgb(1, 0, 0, 0.5),
     border = NA,
     freq = FALSE,
     breaks = breaks,
     main = "Posterior of theta: Before vs After",
     xlab = "mu")

hist(post_redraws[,2],
     col = rgb(0, 0, 1, 0.5),
     border = NA,
     freq = FALSE,
     breaks = breaks,
     add = TRUE)
```

Try the importance sampling with the analytically derived marginal.
```{r}
# Calculate log conditional marginal probability
log_p <- vapply(
  mu_draws,
  FUN = function(mu_i) {
    log_vals <- cond_loglik_m2_true(mu_i)
    log_mean_exp(log_vals)
  },
  FUN.VALUE = numeric(1L)
)

# Calculate importance sampling weights
log_w = -log_p
log_sum_w <- matrixStats::logSumExp(log_w)
w_tilde <- exp(log_w - log_sum_w)

# Re-sample the posterior draws using importance sampling weights
{
  post_draws <- cbind(mu_draws, theta_draws)
  ix <- seq(1, nrow(post_draws))

  ix_redraw <- sample(ix, replace=TRUE, prob=w_tilde)

  post_redraws <- post_draws[ix_redraw,]
}
```

```{r}
cat(sprintf("Full      E[mu]:     %.4f\n", mean(mu_draws)))
cat(sprintf("Corrected E[mu]:     %.4f\n", mean(post_redraws[,1])))

cat(sprintf("Full      V[mu]:     %.4f\n", var(mu_draws)))
cat(sprintf("Corrected V[mu]:     %.4f\n", var(post_redraws[,1])))

cat(sprintf("Full      E[theta]:  %.4f\n", mean(theta_draws)))
cat(sprintf("Corrected E[theta]:  %.4f\n", mean(post_redraws[,2])))

cat(sprintf("Full      V[theta]:  %.4f\n", var(theta_draws)))
cat(sprintf("Corrected V[theta]:  %.4f\n", var(post_redraws[,2])))
```

```{r}
breaks <- pretty(c(mu_draws, post_redraws[,1]), n = 30)

hist(mu_draws,
     col = rgb(1, 0, 0, 0.5),
     border = NA,
     freq = FALSE,
     breaks = breaks,
     main = "Posterior of mu: Before vs After",
     xlab = "mu")

hist(post_redraws[,1],
     col = rgb(0, 0, 1, 0.5),
     border = NA,
     freq = FALSE,
     breaks = breaks,
     add = TRUE)
```
```{r}
breaks <- pretty(c(theta_draws, post_redraws[,2]), n = 30)

hist(theta_draws,
     col = rgb(1, 0, 0, 0.5),
     border = NA,
     freq = FALSE,
     breaks = breaks,
     main = "Posterior of theta: Before vs After",
     xlab = "mu")

hist(post_redraws[,2],
     col = rgb(0, 0, 1, 0.5),
     border = NA,
     freq = FALSE,
     breaks = breaks,
     add = TRUE)
```

```{r}
sigma2_theta <- 0.5      # prior variance of θ (single source of truth)
y2 <- data$y2
n2 <- length(y2)

# Theoretical log p(y2 | μ) using the same sigma2_theta
cond_loglik_m2_true <- function(mu) {
  one_n2 <- rep(1, n2)
  S <- diag(n2) + sigma2_theta * (one_n2 %o% one_n2)
  dmvnorm(y2, mean = rep(mu, n2), sigma = S, log = TRUE)
}

# Monte Carlo log p(y2 | μ) – your code, but using sigma2_theta explicitly
M <- 3000
theta_prior_draws <- rnorm(M, 0, sqrt(sigma2_theta))

cond_loglik_m2 <- function(mu, theta) {
  mean_val <- mu + theta
  sum(dnorm(y2, mean = mean_val, sd = 1, log = TRUE))
}

log_mean_exp <- function(logx) {
  m <- max(logx)
  m + log(mean(exp(logx - m)))
}

log_p_mc <- vapply(
  mu_draws,
  FUN = function(mu_i) {
    log_vals <- vapply(
      theta_prior_draws,
      FUN       = function(th) cond_loglik_m2(mu_i, th),
      FUN.VALUE = numeric(1L)
    )
    log_mean_exp(log_vals)
  },
  FUN.VALUE = numeric(1L)
)

# Theoretical values at the *same* μ's
log_p_true <- vapply(mu_draws, cond_loglik_m2_true, numeric(1L))

# Compare after standardising (kills any affine differences)
log_p_mc_centered   <- (log_p_mc   - mean(log_p_mc))   / sd(log_p_mc)
log_p_true_centered <- (log_p_true - mean(log_p_true)) / sd(log_p_true)

plot(
  mu_draws, log_p_true_centered,
  pch = 16, cex = 0.4, col = "grey40",
  xlab = "mu", ylab = "z-scored log p(y2 | mu)"
)
points(mu_draws, log_p_mc_centered, pch = 1, cex = 0.4, col = "blue")
abline(h = 0, lty = 3)

cat("Correlation: ",
    cor(log_p_true_centered, log_p_mc_centered),
    "\n")

```
